title: Mock Paper 1
description: A full-length practice paper covering core Computer Science units.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a valid data structure operation on a stack?
questions.explanation: A stack supports push and pop operations.

questions.prompt: The process of finding and fixing bugs in software is called:
questions.explanation: Debugging is the process of identifying and correcting defects.

questions.prompt: Which protocol is used for secure remote login?
questions.explanation: SSH provides encrypted remote access to a system.

questions.prompt: A database transaction is atomic if:
options: It completes fully or not at all
questions.answer: It completes fully or not at all
questions.explanation: Atomicity ensures a transaction is treated as a single unit.

questions.prompt: Which component of the CPU performs arithmetic and logical operations?
questions.explanation: The ALU handles arithmetic and logical computations.

title: Mock Paper 2
description: A second full-length mock paper with mixed conceptual MCQs.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a scheduling algorithm?
questions.explanation: Round Robin is a CPU scheduling algorithm.

questions.prompt: The output of the lexical analyzer is:
questions.explanation: Lexical analysis converts source text into tokens.

questions.prompt: Which of the following is a private IP address range?

questions.explanation: Finite automata are used to recognize regular languages.

questions.prompt: Which of the following improves performance by overlapping instruction stages?
questions.explanation: Pipelining overlaps instruction execution stages to improve throughput.
